message:
So last winter, a surprise ice storm rolled in overnight, and I realized—at 6 a.m., in my pajamas—that I had NO idea where our flashlights were. Found one in the garage (dead batteries), another in a kitchen junk drawer (also dead), and what I'm *pretty sure* was a glow stick from 2019 under the couch.

Turns out "I'll figure it out when it happens" is not actually a plan. 😅

Now I keep a little weather-readiness checklist where I can actually *find* it. Nothing fancy—just knowing we have working flashlights, water, a way to charge phones, and blankets that aren't buried in storage. Sounds basic, but that peace of mind? Game-changer.

Have you ever been caught off-guard by a storm and had to do the panic-search dance?
